headline: South Florida's angel investor ecosystem is consolidating while startups like Racquet 360 and Cyrus AI are pushing innovation into new frontiers.

stories.title: Racquet 360 nets $9M for padel expansion across US
stories.summary: Key Biscayne-based Racquet 360 raised $9M to scale padel across America, building out leagues, events, retail, and clubs. Founder Emiliano Abramzon is basically making Miami the padel epicenter while literally betting on the sport's explosive US growth.
stories.takeaway: Padel's lowkey becoming Miami's next fitness empire after Zumba and Barry's—and investors are fr fr backing it.

stories.title: Cyrus AI automates the sales reps' worst nightmare
stories.summary: Miami startup Cyrus AI is building AI that tackles the tedious middle of sales—docs, approvals, follow-ups. Co-founders Ali Zolfaghari and Jake Heenehan want to 4x rep productivity by handling the stuff they actually hate doing.
stories.takeaway: Instead of replacing reps, they're multiplying them with AI that does the manual grunt work sales teams waste time on.

stories.title: Miami Angels and New World Angels merge to form statewide powerhouse
stories.summary: Two major South Florida angel networks combined on Mar 10 to create Florida's premier early-stage investor platform. The merger gives founders single-point access to bigger checks while NWA applies AI to manage a 5x surge in incoming pitches.
stories.takeaway: Miami's angel ecosystem is maturing and consolidating—basically the region's proving it can scale capital infrastructure like legacy tech hubs.

stories.title: USF research spending hits $531M, fueling AI and space innovation
stories.summary: University of South Florida reported $531M in research expenditures for 2025—a 15% bump over two years driven by federal grants and record corporate partnerships. USF is now ranked #2 in Florida and #47 nationally for research spend.
stories.takeaway: Tbh Tampa's quietly becoming a research powerhouse; USF's momentum with NASA, nursing workforce dev, and Alzheimer's research is literally changing the region's tech trajectory.

watchNext.title: Space-Eyes platform detects dark vessels for maritime security
watchNext.why: South Florida's deep-tech scene is expanding beyond padel—this space-based maritime tech startup dropped Mar 11 and shows the region's getting serious about aerospace innovation.

watchNext.title: FAU lands $4.5M Air Force flight simulator from TENCAP
watchNext.why: Defense and aerospace contracts are literally flowing into South Florida universities. FAU's simulator deal (Mar 8) signals federal dollars backing hard tech innovation locally.
